Grading methodology, reporting, and reserve-structure questions.
Later adoption, phase-in reporting, hedging, and the end-of-source boundary.

boundaries: Keep the FAQ separate from the core Regulation 213 wave.
Keep the other amendment-history PDFs in the same folder out of this wave unless a later amendment-history plan explicitly opens them.
Preserve page locators and line references where available, but expect the text layer to remain page-image-backed for exact wording.
Keep the opening, middle, and closing windows separate so the boundary logic stays easy to review.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No Regulation 213 core-wave extraction in this batch.
No other amendment-history PDFs in the same wave.

reviewStandards.caveatOrCompanionGuidance: Treat the FAQ as non-binding companion guidance that helps explain Regulation 213, not as a stand-alone regulation.
reviewStandards.stateRegulation: Treat the source as New York regulatory guidance tied to Regulation 213.
reviewStandards.jurisdictionSpecificRequirement: Treat New York-specific filing, reporting, or implementation language as jurisdiction-specific when it appears.
reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat direct references to regulatory duties as mapped regulatory context, but do not promote the FAQ itself into binding text.
reviewStandards.reportingRequirement: Treat filing, reporting, and submission language as reporting requirements if they appear.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat memorandum, supplement, certification, and recordkeeping language as documentation expectations if they appear.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to VM-20, VM-21, Regulation 126, and the Valuation Manual as mapped context unless the text operationalizes the reference.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title and introductory text as background unless the text imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat each page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat dense or ambiguous FAQ wording as requiring human review unless the page image confirms the wording.

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The Reg-213 Amendment No. 1 FAQ plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
The dedicated batch-definition file must exist and stay synchronized with the plan.
Every planned batch must stay review-only by default.
The source posture must stay explicitly companion-only, not promoted.
The FAQ should remain page-image-backed for exact wording where needed, even though the text layer is readable.
The FAQ must stay separate from the core Regulation 213 wave and from the other amendment-history PDFs in the folder.
futureChecksSuggested: After the wave completes, add review-index and self-review checks plus a proof-of-concept summary reference.
Keep the plan portable if a later amendment-history wave is approved.

operatingNote.sourceType: text-based PDF guidance note with short Q&A entries
operatingNote.citationGuidance: Page locators are the primary anchor; line references are not expected to be stable for this FAQ.
operatingNote.boundaryGuidance: The FAQ should remain visible as companion guidance rather than a promotion signal.
operatingNote.appendixGuidance: No separate appendix was identified; the FAQ format is the source structure.
operatingNote.pageImageWordingBackstop: Keep the page-image wording backstop visible for exact question and answer wording if later review needs it.
